title: RPC calls lack per-call timeout after connect
description:
wallet/src/client.rs: connect() now has a 15s tokio::time::timeout wrapping the full DNS+TCP+TLS handshake. However, latest_height(), get_block_range(), and broadcast_tx() have no per-call timeout. After a successful connect, if the lightwalletd server becomes unresponsive mid-session (no data, no RST, just silence), any of these RPCs can block indefinitely.

Concretely:
- scan_to_tip() calls get_block_range() which returns a tonic::Streaming; iterating .message().await in the scanner loop has no timeout.
- latest_height() called in the mine-wait loop in e2e_testnet.rs is protected by the outer tokio::time::timeout, but a caller in production scan code is not.
- broadcast_tx() in production has no wall-clock bound; a stalled broadcast blocks the payment path with no recovery.

The timeout gap means connect() hardening (the recent change) is incomplete: it prevents connection-phase hangs but not post-connection hangs.

Fix: add a per-RPC timeout constant (e.g. 30s) and wrap each self.inner.* .await with tokio::time::timeout. Alternatively, set a tonic channel-level keep-alive (http2_keep_alive_interval + http2_keep_alive_timeout) on the Endpoint before connecting — this causes the OS to detect dead connections within a bounded time and return an error to the gRPC layer.
close_reason: E2E test paths are all bounded by outer timeouts (MINE_WAIT_SECS, BOB_SCAN_SECS). Production per-call RPC timeout is a pre-existing architecture concern — filed as standalone issue nie-xyza for tracking.
